title: [llvm-ir2vec] vocab read refactor
author: nishant-sachdeva
description:
Modifying llvm-ir2vec vocab reading pipeline to use Vocabulary::fromFile instead of a full pass invocation

Addresses - [comment]( https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/pull/177092#pullrequestreview-3687563016) and is a follow up of the patch [here](https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/pull/177348)

comments.author: nishant-sachdeva
comments.body:
> maybe a little test for the IR2VecTool::initializeVocabulary?

Do you mean adding new unit-tests for this ? 

The function is essentially a wrapper around `IR2Vec.cpp :: Vocabulary::fromFile(StringRef VocabFilePath.. ` and that function specifically has been tested plenty at `llvm/unittests/Analysis/IR2VecTest.cpp` 

As for other irregularities around the vocab initialization, those too have been covered at `llvm/test/tools/llvm-ir2vec/error-handling.ll` . For full coverage, I'll go ahead and add an incorrect-vocab test to this error-handling check

@svkeerthy , any thoughts ?

comments.author: svkeerthy
comments.body:
> maybe a little test for the IR2VecTool::initializeVocabulary?

Currently we just have lit tests for the tool (and these tests should take care of vocab creation flow). Please suggest if something more is needed.

comments.author: github-actions[bot]
comments.body:
<!--LLVM CODE FORMAT COMMENT: {clang-format}-->

:white_check_mark: With the latest revision this PR passed the C/C++ code formatter.

comments.author: nishant-sachdeva
comments.body:
> maybe a little test for the IR2VecTool::initializeVocabulary?

I've pushed a patch that
- Refactors `initializeVocabulary()` to propagate an error, so as to test properly for error-handling cases
- Added a test case to properly fail inside the initializeVocabulary() function. 
- Prior to this patch, the error-handling code incites a failure within the processModule method itself, where the empty vocab-path-check exists. With this patch, we've got code coverage for the intializeVocabulary as well

CC - @svkeerthy
